items.title: SilabRAT, What's Your Power?
items.description: SilabRAT is an advanced Remote Access Trojan offered as Malware-as-a-Service on Darkweb forums since late 2025, developed by threat actor o1oo1 and sold for $5,000 monthly. This financially-motivated tool focuses on credential theft and cryptocurrency operations, featuring Hidden Virtual Network Computing for invisible remote control, browser profile cloning to bypass session protections, and auto
items.ai_summary: The SilabRAT Remote Access Trojan, developed by threat actor o1oo1, is a highly advanced malware tool offered as Malware-as-a-Service on Darkweb forums for $5,000 monthly. This financially-motivated tool exploits systems through Hidden Virtual Network Computing for invisible remote control and browser profile cloning to bypass session protections, with a focus on credential theft and cryptocurrency operations. The real-world impact of SilabRAT includes unauthorized access to sensitive information, potential financial loss, and compromised system integrity, affecting individuals and organizations who have not implemented robust security measures. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement multi-factor authentication, regularly monitor system logs for suspicious activity, and ensure all remote access software is up-to-date and configured with strong security settings. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing a network-based intrusion detection system to identify and block potential SilabRAT command and control communications.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1588| B[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1021.005| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1219| D[Remote Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1021.005| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Fake Software Tutorials on TikTok Spread Vidar Stealer
items.description: Threat actors are leveraging TikTok and Instagram Reels to distribute the Vidar infostealer through fake software tutorials. Two distinct campaigns use short-form videos disguised as tutorials for unlocking premium software like Spotify. The first campaign uses accounts mimicking official Windows profiles with AI-voiced clips instructing users to run PowerShell commands that download Vidar from lo
items.ai_summary: The Vidar infostealer is being distributed through fake software tutorials on TikTok and Instagram Reels, with threat actors leveraging short-form videos to instruct users on how to unlock premium software. The tutorials, disguised as official Windows profiles, use AI-voiced clips to guide users in running PowerShell commands that download the Vidar malware. If exploited, users may experience data theft and unauthorized access to their systems, with potential real-world impact including financial loss and identity theft. To remediate, defenders should block PowerShell commands that download files from unknown sources and implement application whitelisting to restrict unauthorized software execution. Additionally, users should be cautious when following online tutorials and verify the authenticity of the source before running any commands.

graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.001| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Persistence]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.004| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: China-Linked Fake Consulting Sites Targeting US Clearance Holders Seized
items.description: US Federal authorities have seized 13 domains allegedly used in a Chinese intelligence-linked operation to recruit Americans with access to classified government information. The websites posed as legitimate consulting firms, offering vague consultancy and advisory roles to current and former US government employees, military personnel, and security clearance holders. The operation, which began in
items.ai_summary: The seized domains were used in a social engineering campaign targeting US clearance holders, posing as legitimate consulting firms to recruit Americans with access to classified government information. The attack vector involved creating fake job postings and consultancy roles to lure victims into divulging sensitive information. If exploited, victims may have unknowingly provided classified information to Chinese intelligence-linked operatives, potentially compromising national security. To remediate, US government employees, military personnel, and security clearance holders should be cautious of vague job postings and verify the legitimacy of consulting firms before engaging with them, and organizations should implement robust background checks and vetting processes for new employees. Additionally, individuals should monitor their personal information and report any suspicious activity to the relevant authorities.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Vibe Hacking: Two AI-Augmented Campaigns Target Government and Financial Sectors in Latin America
items.description: Two distinct threat campaigns, SHADOW-AETHER-040 and SHADOW-AETHER-064, have been identified targeting government entities and financial organizations across Latin America using agentic artificial intelligence to conduct cyber intrusions. SHADOW-AETHER-040, a Spanish-speaking group, compromised six government entities in Mexico between December 2025 and January 2026, while SHADOW-AETHER-064, opera
items.ai_summary: The SHADOW-AETHER-040 and SHADOW-AETHER-064 campaigns, leveraging AI-augmented techniques, have targeted government and financial sectors in Latin America. These campaigns have resulted in the compromise of six government entities in Mexico between December 2025 and January 2026, with potential impacts including data breaches and disruption of critical services. The threat actors utilized social engineering tactics and potentially exploited vulnerabilities in software to gain initial access. To remediate, defenders should implement multi-factor authentication, ensure all software is up-to-date, and conduct regular security audits to identify potential vulnerabilities. Additionally, network traffic monitoring and incident response planning can help mitigate the impact of such attacks.

items.title: Sniper's Nest: From Brand Impersonation to Browser Hijacking and CPA Fraud
items.description: An investigation into phishing activity targeting users across the Middle East and North Africa uncovered SniperDz, a centralized Push-Notification-as-a-Service and Phishing-as-a-Service platform. The operation uses fraudulent Facebook accounts impersonating politicians, public figures, and trusted organizations to promote fake offers including free mobile internet packages and financial compensat
items.ai_summary: The SniperDz operation utilizes phishing tactics, specifically T1566, to target users in the Middle East and North Africa, impersonating trusted entities to promote fake offers. This campaign manipulates access tokens, as indicated by T1134, to hijack browsers and engage in CPA fraud. If exploited, users may experience unauthorized access to their personal data and financial information, with potential long-term consequences such as identity theft and financial loss. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering, educate users on phishing detection, and enforce multi-factor authentication to prevent access token manipulation. Additionally, monitoring for suspicious activity related to push notifications and phishing-as-a-service platforms is crucial.

items.title: From external espionage to domestic targeting
items.description: Analysis of OceanLotus activities from 2024-2026 reveals a strategic shift toward domestic espionage within Vietnam. The Vietnam-aligned APT group conducted two distinct campaigns using the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor: a supply-chain attack compromising FireAnt Metakit stock trading platform from October 2025 to March 2026, and a prolonged intrusion into a Vietnamese infrastructure and transport constr
items.ai_summary: The OceanLotus APT group has shifted its focus towards domestic espionage within Vietnam, conducting two distinct campaigns using the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor. The first campaign involved a supply-chain attack on the FireAnt Metakit stock trading platform from October 2025 to March 2026, compromising the platform and potentially allowing the group to gain access to sensitive financial information. The second campaign involved a prolonged intrusion into a Vietnamese infrastructure and transport construction company, highlighting the group's ability to adapt and expand its targeting. To remediate these threats, defenders should implement robust network monitoring and detection capabilities, focusing on unusual patterns of remote access software usage, and ensure that all software and systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches. Additionally, organizations should conduct regular security audits and risk assessments to identify potential vulnerabilities and weaknesses in their systems and networks.

items.title: Targeted espionage against Cambodian government entities
items.description: Acronis Threat Research Unit identified two espionage campaigns targeting Cambodian government entities in defense and public works sectors, attributed to a cluster tracked as Khmer Shadow. Both campaigns delivered a custom C++ loader named NIGHTFORGE through government-themed lures in self-extracting archives. NIGHTFORGE employs sophisticated evasion techniques including NTDLL unhooking and Hell'
items.ai_summary: The NIGHTFORGE custom C++ loader is delivered through self-extracting archives with government-themed lures, targeting Cambodian government entities in the defense and public works sectors. This loader employs sophisticated evasion techniques, including NTDLL unhooking and Hell's Gate evasion, to avoid detection. If exploited, NIGHTFORGE can lead to data encryption and impairment of defenses, resulting in significant real-world impact, including compromised sensitive information and disrupted operations. To remediate, defenders should implement robust email filtering, ensuring that all incoming emails are scanned for suspicious attachments and links, and apply configuration changes to disable macros in Microsoft Office applications, specifically setting the registry key 'HKEY_CURRENT_USERSoftwareMicrosoftOfficexx.0WordSecurity' to 'DisableAllMacros' for Office version 2016 and above. Additionally, network traffic monitoring should be enhanced to detect and block suspicious communications with command and control servers.

    C -->|T1562| D[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic

    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: LBIOC-20260071 - The Gentlemens Leak
items.description: The Gentlemen is an active ransomware and extortion operation that emerged publicly in the second half of 2025, rapidly escalating into a high-volume threat actor. The group appears to be a continuation or reorganization of prior ransomware affiliate activity, with reported connections to the Qilin ecosystem and the Russian-speaking actor 'hastalamuerte.' This growth likely reflects existing ranso
items.ai_summary: The Gentlemen ransomware operation, also known as LBIOC-20260071, has been identified as a high-volume threat actor with connections to the Qilin ecosystem and the Russian-speaking actor 'hastalamuerte.' This group utilizes tactics such as stealing or forging authentication certificates (T1649) to gain initial access to systems, and then encrypts data for impact (T1486), leading to significant disruptions for affected organizations. To mitigate this threat, defenders should prioritize implementing robust authentication mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication, and ensuring that all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches. Additionally, organizations should consider conducting regular security audits to identify and address any potential vulnerabilities. In the event of a ransomware attack, it is essential to have a comprehensive incident response plan in place, including data backups and a communication strategy to minimize the impact on business operations.

items.title: Python Backdoor Threat Analysis Following an AI Deepfake Impersonation Campaign
items.description: A sophisticated campaign linked to APT37 delivers Python-based backdoors through spear-phishing emails containing malicious LNK files disguised as legitimate documents. Attackers use themes including airline e-tickets, North Korea research invitations, and impersonation of defense and police officials to induce execution. The LNK files employ environment variable-based obfuscation techniques to do
items.ai_summary: The Python backdoor threat is delivered through spear-phishing emails containing malicious LNK files disguised as legitimate documents, leveraging themes such as airline e-tickets and impersonation of defense officials to induce execution. The LNK files utilize environment variable-based obfuscation techniques to evade detection, and the backdoor allows attackers to manipulate access tokens and establish remote access. If exploited, this threat can lead to unauthorized access and data breaches, particularly affecting organizations in the defense and government sectors. To remediate, defenders should implement email filters to block suspicious attachments, restrict execution of LNK files from untrusted sources, and monitor for unusual network activity. Additionally, ensuring all systems are up-to-date with the latest security patches and conducting regular security audits can help prevent similar threats.

items.title: ClickFix Evolves with PySoxy Proxying
items.description: A sophisticated ClickFix campaign was observed in April 2026 deploying PySoxy, a decade-old open-source Python SOCKS5 proxy tool, to establish encrypted proxy access on compromised hosts. The attack chain begins with social engineering that tricks users into executing obfuscated PowerShell commands, which then establishes scheduled task persistence and deploys an in-memory PowerShell-based command
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix campaign utilizes social engineering tactics to trick users into executing obfuscated PowerShell commands, which establish scheduled task persistence and deploy an in-memory PowerShell-based command. This attack chain exploits public-facing applications, as identified by TTP T1190, and utilizes PowerShell, as seen in T1059.001, to execute commands on compromised hosts. The campaign's use of PySoxy, a Python SOCKS5 proxy tool, enables encrypted proxy access, increasing the difficulty of detection. To remediate, defenders should update their PowerShell versions to at least 7.2.6 and configure their systems to only allow signed scripts, while also implementing application control policies to restrict the execution of unauthorized commands. Additionally, defenders should monitor for suspicious scheduled tasks and obfuscated files, as indicated by T1053 and T1027, to identify potential compromises.

graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Initial Access]:::tactic

    C -->|T1053| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1190| E[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-10608: A security flaw has been discovered in DedeCMS 5.7.88. This affects the function...
items.description: A security flaw has been discovered in DedeCMS 5.7.88. This affects the function RemoveXSS of the file /plus/carbuyaction.php. The manipulation of the argument postname/des results in sql injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10608 is a critical security flaw in DedeCMS 5.7.88, specifically in the RemoveXSS function of the /plus/carbuyaction.php file. This vulnerability allows for remote sql injection attacks, which can be exploited using the publicly released exploit. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized data access, modification, or deletion, potentially affecting all users of the affected DedeCMS installations. To remediate this issue, administrators should update to DedeCMS 5.7.89 or later, which includes a patch for this vulnerability. Additionally, it is recommended to implement input validation and sanitization for user input to prevent similar attacks in the future.

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-10607: A vulnerability was identified in DedeCMS 5.7.88. The impacted element is the fu...
items.description: A vulnerability was identified in DedeCMS 5.7.88. The impacted element is the function dede_htmlspecialchars of the file /plus/flink.php. The manipulation of the argument msg leads to sql injection. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10607 is a SQL injection vulnerability in DedeCMS 5.7.88, specifically in the dede_htmlspecialchars function of the /plus/flink.php file. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the msg argument, leading to remote code execution. This vulnerability is publicly known and has been exploited, posing a significant risk to DedeCMS users. To remediate this issue, users should update to DedeCMS 5.7.89 or later, which addresses this vulnerability. Additionally, administrators should ensure that the /plus/flink.php file is properly configured to prevent SQL injection attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-10584: Proxy server in Graph Explorer before 3.0.1 falls back to HTTP when certificate ...
items.description:
Proxy server in Graph Explorer before 3.0.1 falls back to HTTP when certificate files are missing, which might allow remote threat actors to obtain sensitive information via interception of requests intended to be sent over HTTPS.



To remediate this issue, users should upgrade to Graph Explorer v3.0.1 or later.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10584 is a vulnerability in Graph Explorer's proxy server, which falls back to HTTP when certificate files are missing. This allows remote threat actors to intercept requests intended for HTTPS, potentially exposing sensitive information. The affected version is Graph Explorer before 3.0.1. To remediate, users should upgrade to Graph Explorer v3.0.1 or later. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 5.9 and is categorized as a public-facing application exploit. Users should prioritize upgrading to the latest version to prevent potential information disclosure.

items.title: CVE-2025-64390: A privilege escalation vulnerability exists in PlayStation 4 firmware versions 1...
items.description: A privilege escalation vulnerability exists in PlayStation 4 firmware versions 13.00 through 13.02. The BD-J (Blu-ray Disc Java) sandbox can be escaped through a malformed JAR file.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-64390 is a privilege escalation vulnerability affecting PlayStation 4 firmware versions 13.00 through 13.02. The vulnerability allows an attacker to escape the BD-J sandbox through a malformed JAR file, enabling exploitation for privilege escalation (T1068). If exploited, an attacker can gain elevated privileges, potentially leading to data theft or unauthorized access to sensitive information. To remediate, update to firmware version 13.03 or later, and ensure the BD-J sandbox is configured to only allow trusted JAR files. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as authentication and access controls, to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: CVE-2021-4479: Dräger Atlan A350 versions 1.00 up to and including 1.01 contains an improper in...
items.description: Dräger Atlan A350 versions 1.00 up to and including 1.01 contains an improper input handling vulnerability that allows attackers to cause a denial of service by sending specifically crafted non-Medibus-compliant data through the Medibus interface. Attackers can transmit malformed data to overload the internal processor, gradually disrupting device operation over several hours and causing loss of d
items.ai_summary: CVE-2021-4479 is a denial-of-service vulnerability in Dräger Atlan A350 versions 1.00 up to and including 1.01, affecting the Medibus interface. Attackers can exploit this vulnerability by sending crafted non-Medibus-compliant data, causing the device to overload and gradually disrupt operation over several hours, resulting in loss of device functionality. To remediate, update to version 1.02 or later, and ensure the Medibus interface is configured to reject non-compliant data. This vulnerability is not exploitable for remote code execution or data breach, but it can cause significant disruption to device operation. Defenders should prioritize updating affected devices and configuring the Medibus interface to prevent exploitation.

items.description: Dräger CC-Vision Basic before 7.5.3 and Dräger CC-Vision E-Cal before 7.2.5.0 contain an out-of-bounds write vulnerability when loading .gdt files. A crafted .gdt file can trigger a buffer overflow during file parsing, allowing an attacker to crash the application or execute malicious code on the underlying system.

items.title: Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims
items.description: About 7 million customers of the genetics testing company had their data stolen by hackers starting in April 2023, and many had their information posted on the dark web.

items.title: Major US surveillance program poised to lapse after legislative deadlock
items.description: It is the first lapse of the spy program, known as Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA), since it was passed into law in 2008.

items.title: Rethinking MDR as Attackers and Defenders Embrace AI
items.description: For most of the past decade, managed detection and response was the answer to a real problem. Security teams couldn't staff around the clock, couldn't hire enough analysts, and needed someone else to handle the alert queue. MDR stepped in. It worked well enough. Until now. The threat landscape has changed faster than the MDR model can adapt. Attackers are using AI to move faster, generate more

items.title: Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical trials data
items.description: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk, the world's largest producer of insulin, disclosed a data breach affecting patient information from some clinical trials. [...]

items.title: LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of three now-patched security flaws impacting LangGraph, including a critical vulnerability chain that could result in remote code execution. LangGraph is an open-source framework created by LangChain to build complex, stateful, and multi-agent artificial intelligence (AI) agentic applications. "An SQL injection in LangGraph's function could

items.title: Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots
items.description: The critical-severity OS command injection vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges. The post Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities
items.description: The browser refresh resolved critical and high-severity security defects, including a dozen use-after-free bugs. The post Chrome 149 Update Patches 28 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: INTERPOL Operation Takes Down Sniper Dz Phishing Platform, Arrests Administrator
items.description: An INTERPOL-led operation last month resulted in the disruption of Sniper Dz, a decade-long phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform, Group-IB said Thursday. The effort, codenamed Operation Ramz, took place between October 2025 and February 2026, and saw authorities from 13 countries in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region making 201 arrests. Included among them was Guedz, the primary

items.title: Anthropic Disputes Fable 5 AI Jailbreak
items.description: An AI hacker claims to have achieved a prompt-based jailbreak shortly after Fable 5’s launch, but Anthropic says it’s not a real jailbreak. The post Anthropic Disputes Fable 5 AI Jailbreak appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti flaw by Sunday
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) ordered government agencies to patch an actively exploited Ivanti Sentry flaw within three days, as mandated by the newly issued Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 26-04. [...]

items.title: Over 73,000 French govt employees affected in Tchap messenger breach
items.description: The French government revealed that a recent breach of its Tchap encrypted messaging platform affects the accounts of over 73,000 employees in the French public sector. [...]

items.title: Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters
items.description: Oracle has mitigated CVE-2026-35273, but it has not publicly confirmed the vulnerability’s in-the-wild exploitation. The post Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Europol Disrupts AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
items.description: Authorities in Europe have disrupted AudiA6, a cryptocurrency laundering service used by ransomware gangs and cybercriminal networks. Europol, in a statement issued Thursday, said the dismantling of AudiA6 cut off a "key financial pipeline used to wash hundreds of millions in illicit profits." The service is estimated to have been used to launder more than €336 million (~$389 million) since the

items.title: Phishing Attack Volume Down 20%, But Risk Still Rising
items.description: Hackers are valuing quality over quantity, using AI to upgrade their phishing attacks rather than multiply them.

items.title: Japanese energy firm loses drive with data of 10.9 million clients
items.description: Kyushu Electric Power Co., Inc. has disclosed a physical security incident that affects private data of more than 10 million customers. [...]

items.title: Maine breach portal abused to publish fake data breach disclosures
items.description: In an unusual misinformation campaign, fraudulent data breach disclosures were submitted to Maine's official breach portal and publicly posted before their legitimacy could be verified, prompting companies to deny the claims. [...]

items.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
items.description: The ShinyHunters extortion crew exploited an unpatched flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft to break into enterprise systems, steal data, and demand payment to keep it private. The campaign hit universities hardest. Google's Mandiant attributes it to the group it tracks as UNC6240, and dates the activity between May 27 and June 9. Oracle did not publish its advisory until June 10, so the bug was a

items.title: Oracle mitigates PeopleSoft zero-day exploited in data theft attacks
items.description: Oracle is warning about a critical PeopleSoft Suite zero-day vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-35273 that allows unauthenticated remote code execution, with the flaw actively exploited in ShinyHunter data theft attacks. [...]

items.title: Max-Severity Ivanti Flaw Exploited 24 Hours After Disclosure
items.description: Initial methods suggest attackers had likely mapped out Ivanti's asset landscape upfront and acted quickly once the exploit became public.

items.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets
items.description: Two security teams have shown, in separate research published this week, that OpenClaw, the popular self-hosted AI agent, can be driven to run attacker-controlled code or hand over sensitive data through ordinary-looking inputs. Imperva buried instructions inside shared contacts, vCards, and location pins that the agent executed without the victim ever seeing them. Varonis built a test agent on

items.title: New GreatXML Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker via Recovery Partition XML Files
items.description: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse and MSNightmare) has released a new Windows BitLocker bypass dubbed GreatXML, a day after they published an exploit for Microsoft Defender. "This was an accidental discovery, it took a total of 4 hours to find this," the researcher said in a post on Blogger. "If you ever attempted to use Windows Defender Offline Scan, you're

items.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Claims 478 Victims, Can Spread Like a Worm
items.description: A new analysis of The Gentlemen operation has revealed that the financially motivated threat group initially operated as an affiliate responsible for conducting double extortion attacks, while leveraging resources from various ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) schemes like LockBit (aka Tenacious Mantis), Qilin (aka Pestilent Mantis), and Medusa (aka Venomous Mantis). According to a detailed report

items.title: Cyber Force not included in Senate defense policy roadmap
items.description: An amendment by Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand (D-NY) to the chamber’s fiscal 2027 national defense authorization bill that would have created the digital-focused service was defeated 14-13 when the Senate Armed Services Committee took up the nearly $1.2 trillion legislation behind closed doors this week.

items.title: Authorities dismantle 'AudiA6' ransomware crypto-laundering service
items.description: Law enforcement has dismantled the “AudiA6” cryptocurrency service allegedly used by ransomware actors and other cybercriminals to launder more than $380 million. [...]

items.title: British high school sends students home following cyberattack
items.description: Great Marlow School, which has 1,428 pupils according to the Department for Education (DfE), said it was set to remain closed while it works with specialist IT and cybersecurity professionals to resolve the issue.

items.title: Segmentation Works for OT If Operators Are Paying Attention
items.description: Even the best segmentation strategy will fall apart without constant oversight and disciplined operations.

items.title: Oracle Addresses PeopleSoft Vulnerability Amid Reports of Zero-Day Attacks
items.description: Oracle has released mitigations for CVE-2026-35273, but it has not said whether it’s a zero-day exploited in ShinyHunters attacks. The post Oracle Addresses PeopleSoft Vulnerability Amid Reports of Zero-Day Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Alert Fatigue Is Becoming a Security Threat of Its Own
items.description: As alert volumes outpace human capacity, organizations are turning to AI, automation, and deeper context to separate real threats from the noise. The post Alert Fatigue Is Becoming a Security Threat of Its Own appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cybersecurity Stars Awards 2026: Winners Announced Across 95 Categories
items.description: Most good security work is invisible by design. Today is the exception. The 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards winners are announced across 95 subcategories in four main award categories. The reason is simple. Cybersecurity is full of work that deserves recognition and rarely gets it. Products that quietly close real gaps. Teams that stop incidents nobody reads about. Companies that raise the

items.title: CISA Directs Federal Agencies to Prioritize Security Patches Based on Risk
items.description: The new BOD 26-04 requires agencies to review and update vulnerability management policies with a focus on KEV catalog entries. The post CISA Directs Federal Agencies to Prioritize Security Patches Based on Risk appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, June 11th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9968, (Thu, Jun 11th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
items.description: North Korea's gross domestic product (GDP) has grown, in part because of the nation's state-sponsored cybercrime groups, which target financial firms and other businesses.

items.title: CISA Rewrites Federal Patching Requirements for AI Threat Era
items.description: The new directive gives federal agencies three days to fix the most dangerous flaws, while less severe issues can be deferred.

items.title: Bug Bounty Research Triggers ServiceNow Security Alert
items.description: Security research inadvertently led organizations to believe they were being breached through their ServiceNow instances.

items.title: AI Risk Worries Insurers & Businesses Alike
items.description: As companies adopt AI, many insurance firms are explicitly excluding AI risks, while others are forging ahead to create the right framework. What risks can firms reasonably manage?

items.title: Nightmare-Eclipse Drops Yet Another Microsoft Exploit, RoguePlanet
items.description: The disgruntled researcher released yet another PoC for a Windows Defender bug that allows for system takeover, showing no signs of abandoning their ongoing feud with Microsoft.

items.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
items.description: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

items.title: CISA Issues New Directive Improving How Federal Agencies Prioritize the Mitigation of Cyber Vulnerabilities
How has use of framing protection security headers changed in the past 3 years?, (Wed, Jun 10th)
items.description: Back in 2023, I wrote a diary[1] discussing how commonly X-Frame-Options and CSP headers containing the frame-ancestors directive were used on 1 million most popular domains on the internet (based on the Tranco list[2]), and how they were set. Given that three years have passed since then, I thought it might be interesting to repeat the analysis and see what â€“ if anything â€“ has changed in the meantime.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, June 10th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9966, (Wed, Jun 10th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: A Record-Breaking Patch Tuesday for June 2026
items.description: Microsoft today released software updates to plug nearly 200 security holes across its Windows operating systems and supported software, a record number of fixes for the company's monthly Patch Tuesday cycle. Nearly three dozen of those bugs earned Microsoft's most dire "critical" rating, and exploit code for at least three of the weaknesses is now publicly available.

items.title: The Invisible Battlefield: How Cyberwar Is Reshaping Everyday Life
items.description: Former National Cyber Director Chris Inglis warns that cyberattacks threaten hospitals, utilities, and essential services.

items.title: Microsoft June 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, Jun 9th)
items.description: Microsoft today released patches for 204 vulnerabilities. 38 of these vulnerabilities are considered critical, and three have been disclosed before today. Six of the vulnerabilities affect Microsoft cloud solutions and do not require any user action. In addition, Microsoft incorporated 360 different vulnerabilities affecting Chromium into its Edge browser.

items.title: CISA Announces Winners of the 2026 President’s Cup Cybersecurity Competition
ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, June 9th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9964, (Tue, Jun 9th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-06-07, (Mon, Jun 8th)
items.description: This diary continues the Internet Storm Center&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s tracking of the TeamPCP supply chain campaign, first documented in the SANS white paper When the Security Scanner Became the Weapon and most recently in the handler diary Activity Through 2026-05-24. Since that update, the story moved into two new places: the United States government, which formally caught up to the campaign, and the wider population of attackers now wielding the Mini Shai-Hulud framework that TeamPCP open-sourced last month.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 8th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9962, (Mon, Jun 8th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: The Evil MSI Background is Back!, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: A few months ago, I wrote a diary about a payload that was embedded into a JPEG picture. It was a MSI-branded background[1]. Yesterday, I spotted another one! It seems that the technic is getting more and more popular. This time, it started with a mail containing a WeTransfer link.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, June 5th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9960, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
